news.headline: Global Axial Piston Hydraulic Motors and Pumps market valued at $2.9 billion in 2023, projected to reach $4.7 billion by 2033 at 4.8% CAGR.
news.summary: Allied Market Research released a report on the Axial Piston Hydraulic Motors and Pumps Market, stating it was valued at $2.9 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow to $4.7 billion by 2033, with a CAGR of 4.8% from 2024 to 2033. The growth is attributed to demand from industries like construction, agriculture, and automotive, along with advancements in hydraulic technology and infrastructure projects. The market is also benefiting from the adoption of energy-efficient hydraulic systems and axial piston motors in industrial machinery. The Asia-Pacific region, especially emerging economies, presents significant growth opportunities. Investments in off-highway vehicles and renewable energy applications are also driving market expansion.

news.headline: Coty to present financial progress & strategy at CAGNY 2025. CEO and CFO speak on Feb 19 at 4:00pm EST. Leading beauty company.
news.summary: Coty, a leading beauty company with iconic brands in fragrance, cosmetics, and skincare, announced that CEO Sue Nabi and CFO Laurent Mercier will speak at the 2025 Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference. The presentation is scheduled for February 19th at 4:00pm EST or 10:00pm CET. Coty will discuss its strategy and performance in front of industry analysts. The company's stock is listed on NYSE as COTY and on Paris Stock Exchange as COTY.

news.headline: Entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy predicts Bitcoin could become more common in corporate treasuries as easy money era ends.
news.summary: Entrepreneur and politician Vivek Ramaswamy suggested on Tuesday that Bitcoin (BTC) could see increased adoption as a corporate treasury asset as the era of easy money comes to an end. Ramaswamy, a member of the Republican Party, highlighted in a recent post that as companies and state institutions undergo a reevaluation of their capital investment strategies, Bitcoin may emerge as a more prevalent treasury holding. He emphasized the need for organizations to reconsider their internal hurdle rates for investment in light of the changing financial landscape. Ramaswamy described this potential shift towards utilizing Bitcoin in treasuries as an intriguing trend to monitor. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Tune in to Insight hosted by Haslinda Amin on Bloomberg on 2/19/2025 for the latest updates.
Capacitor bank market to hit $6.8 billion by 2033, from $4.3 billion in 2023, growing at 4.8% CAGR worldwide.
news.summary: The global capacitor bank market, valued at $4.3 billion in 2023, is projected to reach $6.8 billion by 2033, with a CAGR of 4.8% from 2024 to 2033. Factors driving growth include the integration of capacitor banks with renewable energy systems and advancements in smart grid technology. However, high initial investment and maintenance costs may hinder market growth. The expansion of power grid infrastructure offers significant growth opportunities. The report covers various segments like Voltage, Type, Installation, Connection Type, Application, and Region.详Referencing: https://www.alliedmarketresearch.com/request-sample/A31818

Shiba Inu price drops 25% in a month, 55% holders in losses. Long positions surge despite sluggish growth.
news.summary: The cryptocurrency Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) has experienced a relatively uneventful price movement in the past month, but certain technical indicators linked to this popular dog-themed coin have pointed towards a potential recovery. Currently, the second-largest meme coin globally, SHIB has been undergoing a correction, with a decline of more than 3% in the week and approximately 25% in the past month. Data from IntoTheBlock reveals that about 55% of SHIB holders are facing losses currently, and the growth rate of the network has been slow. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

Hepsor AS reports moderately profitable year in 2024 despite economic challenges. Significant new investments made in Baltics & Canada, including handover of new homes & reduction in vacancy rates. Group remains focused on long-term development projects despite short-term financial implications.
news.summary: In 2024, the Group overall reported a modest profit despite the prevailing economic challenges and high interest rates. Transaction activity had started to pick up from the low point in 2023. Despite the tough market conditions, a considerable number of new homes were delivered to customers, along with numerous new leases signed and a decrease in vacancy rates for existing and new commercial properties. The Group also made new investments in the Baltics and Canada. While it may have been more beneficial in the short run for the Group to limit new investments to reduce costs, the nature of development projects lasting 3-5 years prompted them to continue expanding their portfolio proactively despite the challenging economic climate.

This surge is attributed to various factors including the election victory of Donald Trump and the growing demand for spot crypto ETFs. The exact numbers indicate a substantial rise in trading activity, underscoring the influence of these events on the market.

Consilio receives Great Place To Work in India certification for the second year, based on employee survey. At least 70% rated it positively.
news.summary: Consilio, a leading provider of legal technology solutions and enterprise legal services, has been recognized by Great Place To Work® in India for the second year in a row. The certification is awarded based on feedback from an employee survey assessing the employee experience. To qualify for this certification, at least 70% of employees must rate the organization positively. Great Place To Work® is a renowned authority on workplace culture. This recognition highlights Consilio's commitment to fostering a positive work environment for its employees in India.

Howard Hughes Corp confirms receiving revised unsolicited proposal from Pershing Square; specific details undisclosed.
news.summary: Researchers have discovered a significant rise in cyberattacks globally in the last year, with a 25% increase compared to the previous year. This surge in cybercrimes has resulted in approximately 3.5 billion records being compromised. The financial sector was the most targeted industry, accounting for 23% of the total cyberattacks. The healthcare sector followed closely behind with 17% of the attacks. Additionally, there has been a notable increase in phishing attacks, which were up by 47% from the previous year.Experts are advising individuals and organizations to enhance their cybersecurity measures to mitigate the risks associated with these escalating cyber threats.

news.headline: Libra co-creator boasted paying Javier Milei's sister for token post: Report reveals ties to Hayden Davis and memecoin.
news.summary: According to reports from media outlets in Argentina, a person associated with the Libra token, Hayden Davis, has allegedly claimed to have paid an undisclosed amount to Javier Milei’s sister in exchange for information on the memecoin. Davis reportedly boasted about this transaction, indicating a potential attempt to acquire inside details on the cryptocurrency. The specific amount paid and additional details regarding the situation have not been disclosed.

news.headline: Mitsui acquires 40% interest in Rhodes Ridge Joint Venture from Rio Tinto's partners, plans to acquire additional 15%, subject to approvals.
news.summary: In Perth, Australia, Rio Tinto announces Mitsui & Co.'s acquisition of a 40% stake in the Rhodes Ridge Joint Venture (RRJV) from Rio Tinto's partners. Specifically, Mitsui & Co. is purchasing VOC Group Limited's 25% share in the venture and has also signed a heads of agreement to acquire an additional 15% interest from AMB Holdings Pty Ltd. Both transactions are contingent upon regulatory approvals.

news.headline: Business magnate Gupta's Australian steelworks plunged into administration due to financial problems.
DigiFT & Invesco partnering on tokenized solution for investors in Singapore platform, providing access to private credit strategies.
news.summary: DigiFT has partnered with Invesco to provide a tokenized option for accredited and institutional investors through DigiFT Singapore's platform, granting access to one of Invesco's exclusive private credit strategies. This initiative sets the standard as the sole token originating from Singapore that allows daily trading opportunities.

news.headline: Binance CEO discusses how Trump presidency could boost crypto market.
news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 3.8%, the lowest it has been in over a decade. The report also indicates that the economy added 250,000 jobs last month, surpassing expectations. Additionally, the stock market hit a record high, with the main index reaching 30,000 points for the first time. This positive economic news has sparked optimism among analysts and investors, who believe that the country is on track for a strong recovery.

news.headline: VanEck's Matthew Sigel suggests Twitter's Jack Dorsey may be Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto due to evidence like funding legal defenses and interest in cryptography.
news.summary: Matthew Sigel, head of Digital Assets Research at VanEck, stirred up speculation on Tuesday by suggesting that Jack Dorsey, the tech mogul behind Twitter and Block, Inc., could be the mysterious creator of Bitcoin, Satoshi Nakamoto. Sigel clarified that this was his personal opinion and not endorsed by VanEck. He pointed to various pieces of evidence such as technical parallels, circumstantial links, patterns, coincidences, motive, and Dorsey's interest in cryptography. Sigel's research delved into Dorsey's funding of legal defenses against false claims of being Satoshi, his affinity for cryptography and anonymity, his absence during key Bitcoin milestones, and his previous residence. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
